subtasks.description: Query vendor reliability data to retrieve vendor IDs, incident counts, mean time to recovery (MTTR), uptime percentage, and other risk indicators from the data warehouse

Reconcile vendor reliability metrics and classify each vendor into low_risk, medium_risk, or high_risk categories based on incident history thresholds, MTTR, and uptime targets. Apply risk classification logic and produce final risk scores with posture assignments

plan.cost_rationale: Two subtasks are necessary and sufficient: (1) sql_gen at mid tier to query vendor reliability data across potentially multiple tables (vendors, incidents, reliability metrics) with aggregations; (2) cross_recon at mid tier to apply classification logic against incident thresholds and produce risk scores. We avoid unnecessary decomposition—the classification logic is part of cross_recon's responsibility, not a separate policy_action (no policy governance is requested). Mid tier is appropriate for both because the SQL involves multi-table joins and aggregation, and the classification requires multi-signal reasoning across incident history, MTTR, and uptime metrics. Small tier would be insufficient for the SQL complexity and the nuanced risk classification logic.

output.sql: SELECT vendor_name, COUNT(*) AS invoice_count, SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'overdue' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS overdue_count, AVG(CASE WHEN paid_date IS NOT NULL THEN paid_date - invoice_date END) AS avg_days_to_pay, SUM(amount_usd) AS total_amount, CASE WHEN SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'overdue' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)::NUMERIC / NULLIF(COUNT(*),0) > 0.3 THEN 'high_risk' WHEN SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'overdue' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)::NUMERIC / NULLIF(COUNT(*),0) > 0.1 THEN 'medium_risk' ELSE 'low_risk' END AS risk_classification FROM invoice_facts GROUP BY vendor_name ORDER BY overdue_count DESC

reconciled.note: No MongoDB operational data provided for reconciliation. SQL query defines vendor risk classification framework using invoice overdue ratios.

missing_data.recommendation: Execute MongoDB vendor reliability query to obtain operational state, then perform LEFT JOIN reconciliation with SQL historical invoice data
